entities_added.label: Hidden levels progression framework in Email 2
Shift from Email 1 identity disruption to Email 2 progression promise

facts_written.statement: Core positioning metaphor: most people (including wealthy ones) follow pre-programmed scripts like video game NPCs, while 'Players' write their own game code. This is the primary belief shift the sequence sells.
facts_written.evidence: There are only two types of people: NPCs - Following society's code (even the rich ones) Players - Writing their own game code

facts_written.statement: Both emails target the emotional gap between financial success and fulfillment. This is the core pain point used to qualify leads — people who 'won' but feel empty.
facts_written.evidence: I'd won the money game... But I was still following someone else's script.

facts_written.statement: The product being sold: a life-management system using game mechanics (XP, missions, levels, scores) applied to real-world tasks and calendar management.
facts_written.evidence: Where you earn XP for real-world tasks. Where your calendar becomes a mission list. Where every choice either levels you up or keeps you stuck.

facts_written.statement: Scarcity mechanism limiting enrollment to 20 players per month, used in both emails as urgency driver and positioning signal for high-touch delivery.
facts_written.evidence: We only take 20 players per month

facts_written.statement: Primary conversion action across both emails: prospect books a call to see if they qualify. Framed as qualification (they apply to you), not a sales pitch.
facts_written.evidence: Book a call to see if you qualify for The Game of Lyfe

facts_written.statement: Antony positions himself as someone who cashed in on crypto at 22, burned out, spent 2 years building the system, and is now semi-retired at 24. This is the proof-of-concept story.
facts_written.evidence: I spent 2 years building this system after burning out at 22. Now I'm semi-retired at 24.

facts_written.statement: Email 2 reframes the game metaphor as a level system: society shows Levels 1-3 (school/career/retirement), but hidden Levels 4+ exist. Prospect is positioned as stuck between Level 3 and 4.
facts_written.evidence: Right now, you're probably stuck between Level 3 and 4. You can FEEL there's something more. You can SENSE the next level exists. But you can't ACCESS it.

facts_written.statement: Email 1 disrupts identity (you're an NPC). Email 2 offers a progression map (hidden levels beyond success). The sequence moves from pain/awareness to aspiration/solution.
facts_written.evidence: The shift from NPC to Player isn't about working harder or making more money. It's about installing a completely different system.

facts_written.statement: The aspirational outcome sold to the prospect: stop following society's pre-programmed path and design your own life as an intentional game with mechanics you control.
facts_written.evidence: If you're ready to turn your life into a game YOU'RE actually designing...

facts_written.statement: The CTA is framed as the prospect qualifying for the program, not the program selling to them. This is a deliberate positioning constraint that filters for committed leads.
facts_written.evidence: Book a call to see if you qualify for The Game of Lyfe
